label: Monthly financial statements (36+ months)
description: Monthly income statements, balance sheets, and trial balances for the trailing three years plus year-to-date.
buyer_rationale: Buyers rebuild trends month by month; missing or inconsistent statements stall diligence and invite a lower opening offer.

label: EBITDA bridge & add-back schedule
description: A trailing-twelve-month EBITDA bridge itemizing owner comp normalization, personal expenses, one-time costs, and related-party items, each with support.
buyer_rationale: Every add-back a buyer can't substantiate is subtracted from the price; an undocumented recast is the single most common retrade.

label: Reconciliations & AR/AP aging
description: Bank reconciliations for the last twelve months and AR/AP aging as of the two most recent period-ends.
buyer_rationale: Unreconciled books signal that the reported numbers can't be trusted, pushing a buyer toward a full quality-of-earnings review.

label: Sales-tax nexus & exemption certificates
description: States registered for sales tax, the basis for nexus, and any dealer/contractor exemption certificates.
buyer_rationale: Unbooked multi-state sales-tax exposure becomes an indemnity or escrow holdback that reduces cash at close.

label: Inventory policy & costing
description: Inventory counting cadence, valuation/costing methodology, and monthly balances, with how inventory is relieved through COGS.
buyer_rationale: Weak inventory discipline undermines margin reliability and triggers a working-capital-peg fight at close.

label: Accounting system export & tool list
description: An accountant export/backup of the accounting system plus a list of any order-management or finance tools used alongside it.
buyer_rationale: Buyers assess how much of the finance function is systematized versus living in one person's spreadsheets.

label: Supplier list with terms & lead times
description: Purchases by vendor for the last three years with materials supplied, payment terms, lead times, and switching difficulty.
buyer_rationale: Supplier concentration and long lead times are continuity risks a buyer prices the same way as customer concentration.

label: Single-source & critical-component sourcing
description: Critical or single-source vendors, their MOQs and lead times, alternative sources, and any supply agreements.
buyer_rationale: A single foreign or sole-source supplier with no alternate is an enterprise-value risk and a favorite diligence probe.

label: Capacity, bottlenecks & cycle times
description: Actual versus practical capacity, known bottlenecks, and typical cycle times and unit output by product.
buyer_rationale: Buyers underwrite growth against real capacity; undocumented bottlenecks cap the upside they'll pay for.

label: Core-process SOPs & operating KPIs
description: Written SOPs for sales, delivery, invoicing, and vendor management, plus the dashboards/KPIs used to run the business.
buyer_rationale: Undocumented processes mean the business runs on tribal knowledge that may walk out the door post-close.

label: Owner-independence & absence plan
description: What happens to the business if the owner is unavailable for 90 days, and the intended post-close transition plan.
buyer_rationale: Owner dependence is the classic value-killer: a business that can't run without the seller commands a discount and heavy earnout.

label: Employee & contractor census
description: All employees and 1099 contractors with title, responsibilities, manager, compensation, hire date, and key-person flag.
buyer_rationale: The census is how a buyer maps who actually runs the business and what retention will cost.

label: Key-person retention & management depth
description: Which people are key to ongoing operations and whether each core function has a manager who could run it without the owner.
buyer_rationale: Thin management depth concentrated in a few people is a diligence red flag that drives retention packages and holdbacks.

label: Benefit plans, handbook & HR policies
description: Healthcare and other benefit plan summaries, the employee handbook, and any written HR or safety policies.
buyer_rationale: Missing HR infrastructure signals compliance exposure a buyer will have to remediate on day one.

label: Employee disputes & workers-comp
description: Known employee disputes, workers'-compensation issues, and material performance matters to understand pre-close.
buyer_rationale: Undisclosed personnel liabilities surface in diligence and erode trust in every other representation.

label: Customer concentration
description: Revenue by top customers (top one and top five as a share of revenue) for the trailing period.
buyer_rationale: Concentration is the first thing a buyer stress-tests: lose the top account and the multiple compresses.

label: Written customer contracts / MSAs
description: Signed customer contracts, MSAs, or retainers, with remaining term and any change-of-control clauses.
buyer_rationale: Revenue with no contract behind it is treated as at-risk and discounted; change-of-control clauses can transfer to the buyer.

label: Channel & partner relationships
description: On-site relationships with builders, dealers, installers, and retailers, including referral, commission, or exclusivity arrangements.
buyer_rationale: A partner who owns the last mile to the customer is a concentration risk that customer counts alone don't reveal.

label: Customer issues, returns & warranty
description: Material customer issues over the last three years: returns, discounts, payment problems, service and warranty cases.
buyer_rationale: Patterns in customer issues predict post-close churn and warranty cost the buyer would inherit.

label: Marketing systems & analytics access
description: Access to analytics, ad accounts, CRM, and any agency reporting; the demand-generation stack.
buyer_rationale: Buyers verify the demand engine is a repeatable system, not one owner's personal network.

label: Product/SKU list, pricing & BOMs
description: Current product/SKU list with pricing and configuration, plus BOMs and engineering drawings for each product and major component.
buyer_rationale: A buyer needs the product economics and specifications to model margin and confirm the offering is transferable.

label: Testing, certification & rated capacity
description: Product testing, certifications, code compliance, and how rated capacities and safety limits were determined.
buyer_rationale: For any safety-critical product, an unproven certification basis is an existential liability and a hard diligence gate.

label: Warranty & product-liability history
description: Three years of warranty claims by type, frequency, and cost, plus any product-liability claims, recalls, or field failures.
buyer_rationale: Undisclosed product-liability exposure can survive the sale and become the buyer's problem — so it is priced conservatively.

label: Company ownership of intellectual property
description: Confirmation that all patents, trademarks, designs, and domains are owned by the company (not a founder personally) with assignments and maintenance current.
buyer_rationale: IP sitting in a founder's name is a closing condition and a classic value-killer until it is formally assigned to the company.

label: Product roadmap & development pipeline
description: The product development process, open engineering projects, and any new products or variants under consideration.
buyer_rationale: A stalled roadmap weakens the growth story a buyer is paying a forward multiple for.

label: Operating permits, licenses & OSHA
description: Current operating permits, licenses, and registrations, plus any OSHA, safety, or regulatory matters from the last three years.
buyer_rationale: Lapsed permits or open safety matters are compliance liabilities a buyer must clear before or at close.

label: Hazardous materials & environmental
description: Whether the facility handles hazardous materials, and any contamination, notices, remediation, or environmental concerns.
buyer_rationale: Environmental exposure can attach to the buyer and the property, so it is diligenced hard and escrowed.

label: IT environment & software licensing
description: Software used and license terms, email/file storage, domain ownership, cloud versus on-prem, and any outside IT support.
buyer_rationale: Buyers confirm the systems, licenses, and domains transfer cleanly and aren't tied to the seller personally.

label: Insurance policies & loss runs
description: Current insurance policies and several years of loss runs, summarizing material claims and corrective actions.
buyer_rationale: Loss-run history reveals hidden operational risk and tells the buyer what coverage will cost post-close.

label: Litigation, investigations & audits
description: Past or present legal actions, tax audits, government investigations, or threatened claims involving the company or its principals.
buyer_rationale: Any undisclosed proceeding is a representation risk that can unwind trust across the entire transaction.

label: Corporate formation & good standing
description: Articles/certificate of formation, operating agreement or bylaws, and foreign-qualification registrations where applicable.
buyer_rationale: Clean corporate records are the baseline a buyer's counsel needs before they will paper a deal.

label: Cap table & buy-sell agreements
description: Equity ownership records and any shareholder, buy-sell, or equity-incentive agreements affecting ownership rights.
buyer_rationale: Ambiguous ownership or a missing buy-sell among co-owners can block a sale until it is resolved.

label: Liens, encumbrances & debt schedule
description: Documents creating liens, mortgages, or security interests, plus bank/credit arrangements and guarantees.
buyer_rationale: Undisclosed liens and guarantees change what the buyer actually receives and must be cleared at close.

label: Material customer & supplier contracts
description: Copies of all material written agreements with customers and suppliers, and any oral/'soft' commitments.
buyer_rationale: Contracts that don't exist in writing can't be assigned; a buyer treats undocumented relationships as unstable.

label: Employment, non-compete & consulting agreements
description: Employment agreements, non-competes, non-solicits, and consulting agreements for key people.
buyer_rationale: Without non-competes, key employees (and the seller) can walk to a competitor after close — so buyers require them.

label: IP registrations, assignments & licenses
description: Registered and unregistered patents, trademarks, copyrights, and domains, with assignments to the company and any inbound/outbound licenses.
buyer_rationale: This is where a buyer confirms the IP the business runs on is actually owned and freely transferable.

label: Regulatory correspondence & disclosures
description: Reports filed and significant correspondence with state or federal regulators over the last three years, and any material disclosures.
buyer_rationale: A hidden regulatory issue discovered late can derail a deal that was otherwise ready to close.
